session.user:
FIRST check docs/learned/index.md for existing documentation on this topic. Read relevant docs before exploring raw files. Then explore codebase as necessary.
I'm getting "No commits ahead of main. Nothing to submit." from `erk pr submit` even though `git log --oneline origin/master..HEAD` shows 4 commits ahead. The branch is `plnd/hide-erk-reconcile-03-09-2038` and there's already a draft PR #9121.
Investigate the `erk pr submit` command to understand how it determines "commits ahead of main" and why it might fail despite having commits. Look at the submit pipeline code, especially how it resolves the main branch reference. Check src/erk/cli/ for the submit implementation.
session.assistant: I'll help you investigate the `erk pr submit` command and understand why it's not detecting commits ahead of main. Let me start by checking the documentation and then exploring the codebase.
session.tool_result:
1→<!-- AUTO-GENERATED FILE - DO NOT EDIT DIRECTLY --> 2→<!-- Edit source frontmatter, then run 'erk docs sync' to regenerate. --> 3→ 4→# Agent Documentation 5→ 6→Before starting work, scan the read-when conditions below. 7→If your current task matches, read the linked document **before writing code**. 8→ 9→## Categories 10→ 11→- [architecture/](architecture/) — Explore when working on core patterns (dry-run, gateways, subprocess, shell integration). Add docs here for cross-cutting technical patterns. 12→- [capabilities/](capabilities/) 13→- [changelog/](changelog/) 14→- [checklists/](checklists/) 15→- [ci/](ci/) 16→- [claude-code/](claude-code/) 17→- [cli/](cli/) — Explore when building CLI commands or output formatting. Add docs here for Click patterns and terminal UX. 18→- [commands/](commands/) — Explore when creating or optimizing slash commands. Add docs here for command authoring patterns. 19→- [config/](config/) 20→- [configuration/](configuration/) 21→- [documentation/](documentation/) — Explore when structuring or writing documentation. Add docs here for documentation methodology and best practices. 22→- [erk/](erk/) — Explore when working with erk-specific workflows (worktrees, PR sync, Graphite). Add docs here for erk user-facing features. 23→- [erk-dev/](erk-dev/) 24→- [gateway/](gateway/) 25→- [hooks/](hooks/) — Explore when creating or debugging hooks. Add docs here for hook development patterns. 26→- [integrations/](integrations/) 27→- [objectives/](objectives/) 28→- [planning/](planning/) — Explore when working with plans, .erk/impl-context/ folders, or agent delegation. Add docs here for planning workflow patterns. 29→- [pr-operations/](pr-operations/) 30→- [refactoring/](refactoring/)

session.assistant: Now let me read the PR submission documentation:
session.tool_result:
1→--- 2→title: PR Submit Pipeline Architecture 3→read_when: 4→ - "modifying the PR submit workflow" 5→ - "adding new steps to the submit pipeline" 6→ - "debugging PR submission failures" 7→ - "understanding the Graphite-first vs core submit dispatch" 8→tripwires: 9→ - action: "adding a new step to the submit pipeline" 10→ warning: "Each step must return SubmitState | SubmitError. Use dataclasses.replace() for state updates. Add the step to _submit_pipeline() tuple." 11→ - action: "mutating SubmitState fields directly" 12→ warning: "SubmitState is frozen. Use dataclasses.replace(state, field=value) to create new state." 13→ - action: "adding discovery logic outside prepare_state()" 14→ warning: "All discovery (branch name, plan number, parent branch, etc.) must happen in prepare_state() to prevent duplication. Later steps assume these fields are populated." 15→ - action: "using ctx.invoke() with kwargs that don't match target function parameter names" 16→ warning: "Click ctx.invoke() forwards kwargs directly — any name mismatch causes runtime TypeError. Verify all parameter names exactly match the target function signature." 17→ - action: "calling an external tool that overwrites state without capturing it first" 18→ warning: "Save state BEFORE calling external tools that may overwrite it. Reference: capture_existing_pr_body() in submit_pipeline.py captures the PR body before gt submit overwrites it." 19→last_audited: "2026-02-16 14:20 PT" 20→audit_result: clean 21→--- 22→ 23→# PR Submit Pipeline Architecture 24→ 25→The PR submit pipeline is erk's reference implementation of the state threading pattern. This document explains **why** the architecture choices matter, not what the code does (read the source for that). 26→ 27→## Why Linear Pipelines: The Dual-Path Problem 28→ 29→Before the linear pipeline refactor, PR submission used branching orchestration with two paths (Graphite-first vs core). **The problem:** discovery code was duplicated between paths, plan linkage validation happened in multiple places, and error handling varied between branches. 30→
... [152 lines omitted] ...
31→Linear pipelines solve this by **deferring the dispatch decision**. Discovery happens once in step 1, then step 3 dispatches internally to Graphite or core flow. Both paths share the same state type and error handling. 43→3. **Type narrowing** — Later steps assume `branch_name` is `str`, not `str | None`, because step 1 checks and errors if unresolved 67→- **Shared error handling** — Both return `SubmitState | SubmitError`, uniform consumption at CLI layer 84→**Why not fewer steps?** Merging "extract diff + generate description" would make testing harder (can't test diff extraction without running AI generation). Each step represents a **failure boundary** — a distinct phase where errors need different handling. 88→**Decision test:** If the step can fail independently and that failure requires distinct error handling, it deserves to be a separate step. 96→**Why auto-repair instead of erroring?** Early erk workflows created `.erk/impl-context/` but not a reference file. Erroring would break existing worktrees. Auto-repair maintains forward compatibility while migrating to the new structure. 145→## Why dict[str, str] for SubmitError.details 147→<!-- Source: src/erk/cli/commands/pr/submit_pipeline.py, SubmitError --> 149→`SubmitError.details` is `dict[str, str]`, not `dict[str, str] | None` or `dict[str, Any]`. 151→**Why always present, never None?** Every error has details, even if empty `{}`. Making it optional adds ceremony (callers must check `if details is not None`) for no benefit. 153→**Why `str` values, not `Any`?** Error details are for logging and debugging. String values enable consistent serialization (JSON, log files) without type uncertainty. If you need structured data, that belongs in a dedicated error type, not in `details`. 157→The pipeline detects plan implementations via `state.plan_id is not None or state.branch_name.startswith("plnd/")`. The branch-prefix fallback handles the case where `.erk/impl-context/` cleanup occurred before a failed push attempt, leaving `plan_id` as `None` but the branch name still indicating a plan implementation. This drives two auto-behaviors: 168→- [Discriminated Union Error Handling](../architecture/discriminated-union-error-handling.md) — SubmitError as discriminated union

1→--- 2→title: PR Submission Decision Framework 3→read_when: 4→ - "choosing between git-pr-push and pr-submit commands" 5→ - "understanding PR submission workflows" 6→ - "deciding whether to use Graphite or plain git" 7→last_audited: "2026-02-16 14:20 PT" 8→audit_result: edited 9→tripwires: 10→ - action: "submitting PRs" 11→ warning: "Before creating PRs, understand the workflow tradeoffs" 12→ - action: "validating PRs in workflows" 13→ warning: "PR validation rules apply to both workflows" 14→--- 15→ 16→# PR Submission Decision Framework 17→ 18→Erk provides two PR submission workflows with fundamentally different design goals. Choosing the wrong one creates friction. 19→ 20→## The Core Question 21→ 22→**Do you need stack management?** Not "do you have stacks?" but "does this work benefit from Graphite's stack operations?" 23→ 24→### Use `/erk:git-pr-push` When 25→ 26→- **Remote execution context** — GitHub Actions workflows, CI environments where Graphite isn't available 27→- **Single-branch PRs** — no dependencies on other PRs, simple merge to main 28→- **Commit history preservation matters** — you want to keep granular commit messages intact 29→- **Graphite isn't installed** — bare git repos, lightweight environments 30→
... [108 lines omitted] ...
98→- Agent re-runs the command after a failure 100→- Remote execution retries due to transient failures 102→**Without this check:** `gh pr create` would fail with "PR already exists for branch", requiring manual error recovery. 113→- Issue reference extraction failures (`.erk/impl-context/plan-ref.json` wasn't read correctly) 116→**Anti-pattern:** Treating validation failures as user errors — these are system bugs. If `erk pr check` fails after a workflow completes, the workflow's PR construction logic is broken.

1→--- 2→title: PR Submit Workflow Phases 3→read_when: 4→ - "understanding the erk pr submit workflow" 5→ - "debugging PR submission issues" 6→ - "working with AI-generated PR descriptions" 7→ - "understanding plan context integration in PRs" 8→last_audited: "2026-02-16 14:20 PT" 9→audit_result: clean 10→--- 11→ 12→# PR Submit Workflow Phases 13→ 14→The `erk pr submit` command uses a 5-phase workflow to submit PRs with AI-generated descriptions. This document explains each phase and the two-layer architecture. 15→ 16→## Two-Layer Architecture 17→ 18→The workflow has two layers: 19→ 20→1. **Core layer (always runs)**: `git push` + `gh pr create` - works without Graphite 21→2. **Graphite layer (optional)**: `gt submit` for stack metadata - runs when Graphite is available and branch is tracked 22→ 23→## Workflow Phases 24→ 25→| Phase | Name | Description | 26→| ----- | ----------------------------- | --------------------------------------------- | 27→| 1 | Creating or Updating PR | Push changes and create/find PR | 28→| 2 | Getting diff and plan context | Extract diff and fetch plan concurrently | 29→| 3 | Generating PR description | AI generates title and body via Anthropic API | 30→| 4 | Graphite enhancement | Add stack metadata (if available) |
... [106 lines omitted] ...
89→This phase is non-fatal - errors are warnings, not failures.

1→"""Submit current branch as a pull request. 2→ 3→Runs a linear pipeline of functions transforming a frozen SubmitState: 4→1. Prepare state (resolve repo, branch, parent, issue) 5→2. Commit WIP changes 6→3. Push and create/find PR (Graphite-first or core path) 7→4. Extract diff for AI 8→5. Fetch plan context 9→6. Generate AI PR description 10→7. Enhance with Graphite (if applicable) 11→8. 390→ 391→def _core_submit_flow(ctx: ErkContext, state: SubmitState) -> SubmitState | SubmitError: 392→ """Core submit flow: git push + gh pr create.""" 393→ if not state.quiet: 394→ click.echo(click.style("Phase 1: Creating or Updating PR", bold=True)) 395→ 396→ # Check GitHub authentication 397→ is_gh_authed, gh_username, _ = ctx.github.check_auth_status() 398→ if not is_gh_authed: 399→ return SubmitError( 400→ phase="push_and_create_pr", 401→ error_type="github_auth_failed", 402→ message="GitHub CLI is not authenticated. 1→"""Real implementation of git analysis operations.""" 2→ 3→import subprocess 4→from pathlib import Path 5→ 6→from erk_shared.gateway.git.analysis_ops.abc import GitAnalysisOps 7→from erk_shared.subprocess_utils import run_subprocess_with_context 8→ 9→ 10→class RealGitAnalysisOps(GitAnalysisOps): 11→ """Real implementation of Git analysis operations using subprocess.""" 12→ 13→ # ============================================================================ 14→ # Query Operations 15→ # ============================================================================ 16→ 17→ def count_commits_ahead(self, cwd: Path, base_branch: str) -> int: 18→ """Count commits in HEAD that are not in base_branch.""" 19→ result = subprocess.run( 20→ ["git", "rev-list", "--count", f"{base_branch}..HEAD"], 21→ cwd=cwd, 22→ capture_output=True, 23→ text=True, 24→ check=False, 25→ ) 26→ if result.returncode != 0: 27→ return 0 28→ count_str = result.stdout.strip() 29→ if not count_str: 30→ return 0

1→"""Real implementation of git analysis operations.""" 2→ 3→import subprocess 4→from pathlib import Path 5→ 6→from erk_shared.gateway.git.analysis_ops.abc import GitAnalysisOps 7→from erk_shared.subprocess_utils import run_subprocess_with_context 8→ 9→ 10→class RealGitAnalysisOps(GitAnalysisOps): 11→ """Real implementation of Git analysis operations using subprocess.""" 12→ 13→ # ============================================================================ 14→ # Query Operations 15→ # ============================================================================ 16→ 17→ def count_commits_ahead(self, cwd: Path, base_branch: str) -> int: 18→ """Count commits in HEAD that are not in base_branch.""" 19→ result = subprocess.run( 20→ ["git", "rev-list", "--count", f"{base_branch}..HEAD"], 21→ cwd=cwd, 22→ capture_output=True, 23→ text=True, 24→ check=False, 25→ ) 26→ if result.returncode != 0: 27→ return 0 28→ count_str = result.stdout.strip() 29→ if not count_str: 30→ return 0

session.assistant:
Perfect. Now I have the complete picture. Let me compile the findings:
## Investigation Report: `erk pr submit` "No commits ahead" Error
I've identified the root cause of your issue. The problem is a **chain of silent failures in branch resolution** that causes the submit command to check for commits against a non-existent branch.
### Root Cause
The `erk pr submit` command fails at line 409-416 of `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/pr/submit_pipeline.py` in the `_core_submit_flow()` function:
```python commit_count = ctx.git.analysis.count_commits_ahead(state.cwd, state.parent_branch) if commit_count == 0: return SubmitError( phase="push_and_create_pr", error_type="no_commits", message=f"No commits ahead of {state.parent_branch}. Nothing to submit.", details={"parent_branch": state.parent_branch, "branch": state.branch_name}, ) ```
The issue is that `state.parent_branch` is being set to a branch name that **doesn't exist locally**, so when `count_commits_ahead()` tries to count commits, it silently returns 0 instead of detecting commits.
### The Silent Failure Chain
**Step 1: Trunk branch detection fails silently** (`prepare_state()`, line 156) - Calls `ctx.git.branch.detect_trunk_branch(repo_root)` - This tries: `git symbolic-ref refs/remotes/origin/HEAD` (fails - no symbolic ref) - Then tries: `git show-ref --verify refs/heads/main` (fails - main doesn't exist locally) - Then tries: `git show-ref --verify refs/heads/master` (fails - master doesn't exist locally) - **Final fallback: returns hardcoded string "main"** (line 215 of `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/git/branch_ops/real.py`)
**Step 2: Parent branch detection returns the non-existent trunk branch** (`prepare_state()`, line 157) - Calls `ctx.branch_manager.get_parent_branch(repo_root, branch_name)` - Your branch manager is `GitBranchManager` (no Graphite tracking), which returns `None` (line 154 of `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/branch_manager/git.py`) - The code then uses: `parent_branch = ... or trunk_branch` - So `parent_branch = "main"`
**Step 3: Commit counting against non-existent branch returns 0** (`count_commits_ahead()`, line 17-31) - Calls `git rev-list --count main..HEAD` - This fails because "main" doesn't exist locally - The error is silently caught and returns 0 (line 26-27)
**Step 4: The error message you see** - The pipeline checks if `commit_count == 0` and returns the "No commits ahead" error
### Why This Happens on Your Branch
Your repository situation: - **Local branches**: Only `plnd/hide-erk-reconcile-03-09-2038` exists (your current branch) - **Remote branch**: `origin/plnd/hide-erk-reconcile-03-09-2038` exists - **Remote trunk**: `origin/master` exists - **Local trunk**: Neither `main` nor `master` exist locally - **Git symbolic ref**: Not set (no `refs/remotes/origin/HEAD`)
Your branch tracks `origin/plnd/hide-erk-reconcile-03-09-2038` (set up by git push), but there's no local `main` or `master` branch to serve as parent/trunk.
### The Bug
The real problem is in the `count_commits_ahead()` function. When `git rev-list --count <nonexistent-branch>..HEAD` fails, it silently returns 0 instead of: 1. Distinguishing between "0 commits" and "branch doesn't exist" 2. Using the remote trunk branch as a fallback when the local trunk doesn't exist
### Correct Behavior
The command should: 1. Detect that `origin/master` exists (the actual remote trunk) 2. Count commits against `origin/master` when the local `main`/`master` doesn't exist 3. Return "4 commits ahead" (as your manual `git log origin/master..HEAD` shows)
### Supporting Evidence
From your environment: ``` $ git log --oneline origin/master..HEAD b8ddb5aa6 WIP: Prepare for PR submission 3bcfea65e Remove plan staging dirs before implementation 249cfa526 Update plan: Make erk reconcile a Hidden Command 733792deb One-shot: make erk reconcile a hidden command ```
All 4 commits are ahead of `origin/master`, but the command checks against the non-existent local "main" branch and finds 0 commits.
### Files Involved
- **Primary bug**: `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/git/analysis_ops/real.py` (lines 17-31) - `count_commits_ahead()` silently returns 0 on git error - **Secondary issue**: `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/git/branch_ops/real.py` (lines 182-215) - `detect_trunk_branch()` has a hardcoded fallback to "main" that may not exist - **Tertiary issue**: `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/pr/submit_pipeline.py` (lines 409-416) - Doesn't handle the case where the parent branch doesn't exist
### Workaround
Until this is fixed, you can work around it by creating a local `master` branch that tracks `origin/master`:
```bash git branch --track master origin/master ```
Then `erk pr submit` will count commits against your local `master` branch (which exists and is synced with remote).
